title: Was the referee unfair to Messi’s team? Argentina coach Lionel Scaloni reveals ‘reality’ about World Cup final
content:
• Slovenian referee Slavko Vincic previously officiated Argentina's shocking loss to Saudi Arabia in the 2022 World Cup.
• Spain held a flawless record under referee Slavko Vincic, which included their Euro 2024 semi-final win over France.
• Argentina managed only 2 attempts across the entire final, with Lionel Messi's effort in the 117th minute being their first shot in regulation time.

title: Union ministers J P Nadda, Jitendra Singh visit Sonam Wangchuk at Medanta hospital: Report
content:
• Union Health Minister JP Nadda and Minister of State in the Prime Minister's Office Jitendra Singh visited activist Sonam Wangchuk at Medanta Hospital in Gurugram.
• The Delhi High Court ordered the immediate transfer of activist Sonam Wangchuk from Delhi's Safdarjung Hospital to Medanta Hospital in Gurugram.
• The Delhi High Court observed that allowing Sonam Wangchuk's transfer would safeguard his fundamental rights under Articles 19 and 21 of the Constitution.

title: ‘Messi is a human being’: Sourav Ganguly defends Leo after Argentina’s defeat against Spain in World Cup final
content:
• Spain defeated Argentina 1-0 in extra time in the World Cup final, which took place on 20 July at the New York New Jersey Stadium.
• Lionel Messi, referred to as 39 years old by some critics, struggled for influence in the World Cup final.
• Argentina had successfully defended their Copa América title two years prior to the discussed World Cup final.

title: Liverpool FC owner in talks to sell  ₹57,800 Crore stake to Mittal-backed group: Report
content:
• Liverpool FC's American owners are reportedly in discussions to offload a minority stake in a deal that could value the club at more than ₹57,800 crore (£4.7 billion) to an investment group fronted by Amit Bhatia.
• Fenway Sports Group, led by American billionaire John Henry, acquired Liverpool FC for £300 million in 2010.
• Recent high-value transactions in English football include the around ₹29,500 crore (£2.5 billion) takeover of Chelsea FC in 2022 and Jim Ratcliffe's 2023 acquisition of a minority stake in Manchester United, which valued the club at more than ₹54,810 crore (USD 6.3 billion).

title: Congress surprised its own MPs, Centre with protest at 7 Lok Kalyan Marg | Report explains how
content:
• Congress leaders, including Rahul Gandhi and Priyanka Gandhi Vadra, staged a protest at 7 Lok Kalyan Marg, the official residence of Prime Minister Narendra Modi.
• The protest demanded the resignation of Prime Minister Modi and Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan, also insisting on a discussion in Parliament regarding police actions affecting students.
• During confrontations at the protest, over 118 police personnel were reported injured, and key leaders like Rahul Gandhi and Priyanka Gandhi Vadra were forcibly removed and detained.

title: Strait of Hormuz vessel crossings fall as US-Iran attacks intensify and Houthi blockade threats disrupt shipping: Report
content:
• Only four commodity vessels transited the Strait of Hormuz on Monday, a decrease from seven vessels the previous day, according to Kpler shipping data.
• The United Kingdom Maritime Trade Operations (UKMTO) agency reported on Tuesday (July 21) that a tanker was struck by an unknown projectile while transiting the Strait of Hormuz.
• Greek shipping company Dynacom Tankers stated that two of its managed vessels were hit by projectiles off the coast of Oman on Monday, and a third tanker was struck by a drone at Russia's Novorossiysk CPC terminal in the Black Sea.

title: Privatise all CPSEs; bring oil, gas, alcohol under GST: US think tank wishlist for India
content:
• The Centre for Strategic and International Studies (CSIS) recommended reducing government equity in public sector banks to 33 per cent.
• The CSIS suggested bringing petroleum products, alcohol, real estate, and electricity within the ambit of the Goods and Services Tax (GST).
• The Indian government has completed three reforms from the CSIS list: passage of the Jan Vishwas Bill 2.0, opening up the nuclear sector for private participation, and rationalising Goods and Services Tax (GST) slabs.

title: RBI issues draft for future-ready rules related to foreign investments
content:
• The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) has proposed draft rules for foreign investments, following Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman's announcement in the Union Budget 2026-27 for a comprehensive review.
• The planned revisions update the Foreign Exchange Management (Non-Debt Instruments) Rules, 2019, aiming for rationalisation, harmonisation, and a simplified regulatory architecture.
• The draft rules include provisions for public companies to issue equity on international stock exchanges and permit NRIs/OCIs to subscribe to the National Pension System (NPS) with repatriable savings; comments are invited by August 31.

title: India aims at 10 pc share in global merchandise exports by 2047: WTO report
content:
• India aims to increase its share of global merchandise exports from about 1.8% in 2024 to around 10% by 2047, according to a WTO report.
• The eighth review of India's trade policies and practices will take place on July 21 and July 23 in Geneva, attended by Commerce Secretary Rajesh Agarwal.
• India modified its basic customs duties structure in the 2025-2026 Budget, eliminating the highest rates (100%, 125%, 150%) and reducing applied rates to eight on industrial goods.

title: PPI vs WPI: What is Producer Price Index and why will it replace Wholesale Price Index in India?
content:
• The Wholesale Price Index (WPI)'s base year has been revised from 2011-12 to 2022-23, and its basket expanded from 697 to 957 items.
• The Producer Price Index (PPI) will run in parallel with the WPI for five years before the WPI is discontinued.
• Unlike WPI, the PPI includes the services sector, initially covering seven sectors: banking, securities transactions, insurance, pension fund management, railways, air passenger transport, and telecom.
